title: Invalid and Empty Candidate Examples
description: Demonstrates various invalid and edge-case shapes that are rejected by the contract parser. These ensure fail-closed behavior at parse time.

rejectedExamples.label: Empty bindings object
rejectedExamples.reason: A catalog with no bindings provides no resolution targets.

rejectedExamples.label: Empty candidate chain (v2)
rejectedExamples.reason: A candidate chain must have at least one candidate to resolve.

rejectedExamples.label: Candidate chain in v1 catalog
rejectedExamples.reason: Candidate chains are only supported in version 2 catalogs.

rejectedExamples.label: Whitespace-only model name
rejectedExamples.reason: Model name must be a non-empty trimmed string.

rejectedExamples.label: Invalid binding id (camelCase)
rejectedExamples.reason: Binding ids must be kebab-case: lowercase, digits, hyphens, dots, underscores only.

rejectedExamples.label: Missing model field
rejectedExamples.reason: Every candidate must specify a model.

rejectedExamples.label: Retry policy with zero attempts
rejectedExamples.reason: attemptsPerCandidate must be a positive integer >= 1.

rejectedExamples.label: Unknown binding-level keys
rejectedExamples.reason: Single-model bindings only allow model, declaredCapabilities, and notes.

notes: All the above inputs are rejected at parse time by parseGoalModelBindingCatalog. This fail-closed design ensures invalid bindings are never accidentally used in resolution.
